title: Dana M. Britton (2007-2011)
abstract: Gender & Society started in 1986, the same year that I received my PhD. When I applied to edit the journal in 2004,1 wrote that I saw myself as a beneficiary of the journal, and that I wanted to pass on those benefits to the next generation. I was eager to continue the work of my predecessors by nurturing and encouraging new feminist scholarship and by publishing the best social science research on gender. If I have a legacy, I hope that is it.
